exploration_log.aha_moment: The static $cache property is set once and never invalidated. Adding a static $cacheTime and comparing against the TTL in the cache-hit condition is the minimal change to support TTL-based expiry.
exploration_log.hindsight: Changing getGitInfo from static to instance method is the cleanest way to access $this->ttl without making TTL a static property (which would create issues when multiple instances with different TTLs are used).

alternatives: Make TTL a static class property (last-write-wins)
Pass TTL as argument to static getGitInfo
Convert to instance method and instance cache
key_decisions.decision: Keep static cache/cacheTime for shared efficiency, change getGitInfo to non-static to access instance $ttl
key_decisions.reasoning: Static cache still avoids redundant shell_exec calls across multiple instances. Non-static method allows each instance to apply its own TTL setting without interfering with others.

search_sequence.result: Found static $cache checked in static getGitInfo with no time-based expiry. $cacheTime not tracked anywhere.

solve_notes: Added `$ttl` instance property and parameter (default 0 = cache forever) to GitProcessor. Added static `$cacheTime` to track when cache was last populated. Converted `getGitInfo` from static to instance method so it can access `$this->ttl`. The cache is now invalidated when TTL > 0 and `time() - cacheTime >= ttl`. Updated the doc entry to describe the new parameter and warn about stale data in daemons.
task_text:
Fix GitProcessor caching stale branch and commit data in long-running processes

`GitProcessor` in `Processor/GitProcessor.php` caches the git branch
and commit hash in a static class property (`$cache`) that is
populated once via `git log` and `git branch` shell commands and
never refreshed. In long-running processes (queue workers, daemons,
Swoole servers), the cached data becomes stale when new commits are
pushed to the repository, causing all log records to report an
outdated commit hash. Fix `GitProcessor.php` to accept an optional
TTL parameter that expires the cache after a configurable interval,
triggering a re-read of git data. Also update
`doc/02-handlers-formatters-processors.md` to document the new TTL
parameter and add a warning about stale data in long-running
processes.
